name: The vineyards and villages of the Ebro River Bank – circular from Haro
name_original: Los viñedos y pueblos de la Ribera del Ebro – circular desde Haro
region_intro: Touring cycling around Haro, nestled in the La Rioja region of Spain, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The area is characterized by extensive vineyards, the Ebro River, and the Obarenes Hills, providing varied terrain. Routes often wind through villages and along historic paths, including sections of the Camino Naturales Ebro. This region provides a mix of mostly paved surfaces and some unpaved segments, suitable for various fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Haro The most popular touring cycling route is The vineyards and villages of the Ebro River Bank – circular from Haro, a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the region's renowned wine country. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Anguciana – Haro loop from Haro, a moderate 32.4 miles (52.2 km) path. This route offers varied scenery through the local countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Haro – View of Peña del Buradón loop from Haro, a 25.2 miles (40.6 km) trail leading through the region with views towards Peña del Buradón, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Haro is defined by extensive vineyards, the Ebro River, and the Obarenes Hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Haro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Haro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Haro?
A: Haro offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 70 different options to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy rides to more challenging journeys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Haro?
A: The terrain around Haro is diverse, characterized by extensive vineyards, the scenic Ebro River, and the rolling Obarenes Hills. You'll find a mix of mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels, as well as some unpaved segments for those seeking a more adventurous ride. Routes often wind through charming villages and along historic paths.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Haro?
A: Yes, Haro has several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro – Haro loop from Haro&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 10 km long with minimal elevation gain, offering a pleasant ride through the local area.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with wine tasting in Haro?
A: Absolutely. Haro is renowned as the wine capital of La Rioja Alta, and many cycling routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994435543/the-vineyards-and-villages-of-the-ebro-river-bank-circular-from-haro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vineyards and villages of the Ebro River Bank – circular from Haro&lt;/a&gt;, wind directly through picturesque vineyards. You can easily plan stops at centuries-old bodegas (wineries) to experience the region's famous Rioja wines.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available from Haro?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Haro are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994435543/the-vineyards-and-villages-of-the-ebro-river-bank-circular-from-haro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vineyards and villages of the Ebro River Bank – circular from Haro&lt;/a&gt;, offering a scenic loop through the wine country.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or attractions along the cycling paths?
A: Cycling routes around Haro offer numerous points of interest. You can enjoy views towards Peña del Buradón on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro – View of Peña del Buradón loop from Haro&lt;/a&gt;. Other attractions include the Roman bridge over the Tirón River near Cihuri, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5557101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguciana&lt;/a&gt; or the Paraje de San Felices.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Haro?
A: While touring cycling is enjoyable throughout much of the year, many visitors find June and August to be particularly pleasant months for exploring the routes around Haro. The weather is generally favorable for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully appreciate the vineyards and landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Haro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Haro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 140 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied scenery, the well-maintained paths through vineyards, and the opportunities to explore charming villages and historic sites.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for more experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguciana – Haro loop from Haro&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial distance of over 50 km with varied scenery. Some routes also include unpaved segments and significant elevation gain, requiring good to very good fitness.

Q: Are there any parks or green spaces accessible by bike in Haro?
A: Haro features several green spaces that can be enjoyed during or after your ride. The Vega Gardens offer a pleasant urban area for strolls, while Fuente El Moro Park, located on the road to Anguciana, provides picnic tables and a fountain. Iturrimurri Park offers views of the Ebro River and an old railway line, perfect for a relaxing stop.

Q: Can I find routes that follow historic paths or rivers in Haro?
A: Yes, Haro's location is ideal for exploring historic paths and riverbanks. Sections of the Camino Naturales Ebro (GR 99) pass through Haro, offering opportunities for longer cycling adventures along the Ebro River. You can also find routes that follow the Tirón River, such as the Vista Alegre Walk, which traces the path of the old 'El Bobadilla' railway.
tips:
Haro is the wine capital of La Rioja Alta and the nerve center of wine tourism in Spain. Formerly, livestock and agriculture coexisted with vineyards on its lands. Its particular climatic conditions attracted French winegrowers who were escaping from phylloxera, which caused a total migration of any activity to the development of current infrastructure.

Haro is the birthplace of many of the most famous wineries of the Rioja designation of origin. Its numerous monuments testify to the historical importance of this region.
It is the typical castle-fortress raised on a hill, an old memory of the early medieval rock castle, with a defensive function.
This stretch of the Ebro nature trail runs through an extensive area of vineyards. The dry landscape contrasts with the humidity of the stream and creates an exciting postcard.
